behavior_summary: This script primarily provides small method wrappers and utility routines for an in-game item/actor. The principal exported behavior (METHOD::0080) simply forwards hit/damage events into the underlying item implementation (Item::receiveHit).

Helper routines implement utilities: compute direction to coordinates, present choice prompts to a player/process and wait for a reply, toggle or ensure status bits, update animation frames, and run movement/step decision logic that tests candidate positions with Item::legal_move and issues Item::move when appropriate.

One helper (METHOD::0676) appears to handle a terminal condition: if a specific status bit and containment condition hold it emits a farewell string (Item::bark/suspend) and spawns a BODYPART process, inferred as a death/dismemberment action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_METHOD::METHOD::0080 is a straight-through wrapper: push parameters (this, three uword args) plus an implicit byte 0x01, call Item::receiveHit, return immediately (no suspends or loops).
Several helpers use suspend/wait semantics around Item::ask and around movement checks (movement helpers repeatedly suspend and call Item::legal_move to probe candidate points).

Movement helpers perform multi-step arithmetic (delta, normalization, div/mod, clamping) and loop until a legal move or failure is found; they use FREE::1C52 as a numeric conversion helper in those calculations.
State-manipulation helper (METHOD::0113) branches based on Item::getStatus and either calls Item::orStatus or Item::andStatus to set/clear bits.
open_questions: Exact semantics and full parameter meanings for the movement helpers (METHOD::013E, METHOD::03CD) — e.g., whether the 5th byte parameter is step size, stride, or a direction flag — are inferred but not certain.
Precise behavior of FREE::1C52: it appears to be a numeric conversion/normalization helper used extensively in movement math, but its exact conversion rules are unknown.
Whether METHOD::0676 is invoked only on death or also on other detachment/containment transitions (the triggering conditions are inferred from a tested 0x08 status bit and root container check).
Whether any helper functions are externally exposed as item events (beyond METHOD::0080) or remain internal utility methods called by a larger AI/engine system.

main_function.name: U8P_METHOD::0080
main_function.purpose: Thin wrapper that forwards a received hit/damage event to the item implementation by calling Item::receiveHit with the provided parameters and an implicit byte flag (0x01).
entry_conditions: Called when this method receives a hit/damage event (inferred from its call to Item::receiveHit).
No fast-area enter/leave markers present; behaves as an event handler wrapper.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Forward hit/damage events to Item::receiveHit (thin wrapper).

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::0094
function_map.role: Compute direction to given coordinates by calling Item::getDirToCoords and returning the result.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::00A9
function_map.role: Present a choice list / dialog via Item::ask, suspend to wait for response, process and return the selection string/result.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::0113
function_map.role: Toggle/ensure/clear status bits on the item using Item::getStatus then Item::orStatus or Item::andStatus accordingly.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::013E
function_map.role: Complex movement/position computation and legality checking routine that probes candidate positions and may call Item::legal_move or Item::move; likely used for NPC/item pathing.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::03CD
function_map.role: Variant movement/step routine similar in purpose to METHOD::013E (movement decision and execution).

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::0610
function_map.role: Frame/animation updater: inspects Item::getFrame bits and calls Item::setFrame to adjust animation state.

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::0676
function_map.role: Conditional death/special-state handler: checks a status bit and root container, then barks a farewell message and spawns a BODYPART process (inferred death/dismemberment behavior).

external_calls.purpose: Deliver incoming hit/damage event to the underlying item implementation (parameters forwarded including implicit byte 0x01).
Return direction from this item to supplied coordinates.
Present a slist/choices to a process/player and suspend to wait for the response; returns a string/result.
Read current status bits to decide whether to set or clear bits.
Clear specified status bits when the test indicates they are not set (ensures bits cleared).
Set specified status bits when the test indicates they should be set (ensures bits set).
Obtain current X coordinate used in movement/delta calculations.
Obtain current Y coordinate used in movement/delta calculations.
Obtain current Z coordinate used in movement/delta calculations.
Perform numeric conversion/normalization used repeatedly in movement arithmetic (inferred).
Test whether a candidate WorldPoint is a legal move (collision/terrain check) before moving.
Execute an actual move to validated coordinates with a direction/flag byte.
Probe candidate positions for legality as part of movement variant logic.
Issue movement when a candidate is acceptable.
Read current animation/frame word to compute next frame.
Set updated animation/frame value.
Check the specific 0x08 status bit prior to performing farewell/spawn actions.
Verify containment/root conditions before barking and spawning a BODYPART.
Emit a farewell message string to the associated pid/process (suspends after bark).
Spawn a body-part process/object as part of the terminal action (inferred dismember/ death effect).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script primarily provides small method wrappers and utility routines for an in-game item/actor. The principal exported behavior (METHOD::0080) simply forwards hit/damage events into the underlying item implementation (Item::receiveHit).
